nodes.label: AI as proofreading assistant
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable for tricky grammar
nodes.grounding_span: use AI to proofread, answer grammar questions

nodes.label: AI adaptation to audience and market nuances
nodes.label_negative: AI as overly academic and not simple enough
nodes.grounding_span: generate really simple definitions for vocabulary words

nodes.label: AI as implementation executor
nodes.label_negative: AI as deviating from requested format
nodes.grounding_span: list out very specific instructions for the AI, and it doesn't produce the content in the way I requested

nodes.label: AI as ideation partner
nodes.label_negative: AI as biased to agree with user
nodes.grounding_span: I feel as though it lends itself to agree with me, rather than being critical

nodes.label: AI as tool requiring human oversight
nodes.grounding_span: AI needs to be managed and used critically in order to optimize its results

nodes.label: AI as thought partner for logic checking
nodes.label_negative: AI as inappropriate for generating whole essays
nodes.grounding_span: using it to quickly check grammar or provide examples is a great use of it, but using it to generate whole essays or chunks of text might not be

nodes.label: Data accuracy and integrity
nodes.grounding_span: make sure that the basic integrity of the word is still accurately described

nodes.label: Productivity and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: let the AI do as much as it can before I take over

nodes.label: Personal autonomy and skill preservation
nodes.grounding_span: I do try to challenge myself to not hand over every task I can to AI

nodes.label: Frustration with AI errors
nodes.grounding_span: When I take the time to list out very specific instructions for the AI, and it doesn't produce the content in the way I requested, that can be frustrating

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's agreeableness
nodes.grounding_span: I feel as though it lends itself to agree with me, rather than being critical

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as a helpful tool
nodes.grounding_span: using it to quickly check grammar or provide examples is a great use of it

nodes.label: Optimism about AI as future tool
nodes.grounding_span: I think I'll get better at writing meaningful prompts to get the most out of my work with AI

nodes.label: Iterative prompt refinement and manual takeover
nodes.grounding_span: I'll try different prompts like simplify the syntax or swap out difficult vocabulary words with more basic terms but sometimes it's just easier for me to manually make those changes

nodes.label: Strategic reframing to elicit objectivity
nodes.grounding_span: I find myself having to trick it into being objective by wording questions like, What could be done to make this sentence clearer?
